colors.guidelines: Use 'Organic & Earthy' archetype. Primary Green (#2E7D32) for main actions. Harvest Yellow (#F9A825) for highlights. Background should be Pale Green (#F1F8E9) to reduce glare outdoors.

buttons.primary: bg-[#2E7D32] rounded-full py-4 px-6 items-center justify-center active:opacity-90
buttons.secondary: bg-[#F9A825] rounded-full py-4 px-6 items-center justify-center active:opacity-90
buttons.outline: border-2 border-[#2E7D32] rounded-full py-4 px-6 items-center justify-center

cards.base: bg-white rounded-2xl p-5 shadow-sm border border-green-50/50

inputs.base: bg-white border border-gray-200 rounded-xl p-4 text-lg text-gray-900

layout_strategies.dashboard: Bento Grid: Mixed size cards. Key metrics (Weather/Crop) take 2x1 or 2x2 spans.
layout_strategies.forms: Wizard style. One question per screen or grouped by context. Large inputs.
layout_strategies.lists: Card-based lists with large thumbnails and bold titles.
layout_strategies.navigation: Bottom Tab Bar with labeled icons. Avoid Hamburger menus.

instructions_to_main_agent: CRITICAL: The project is React Native Expo.
1. INSTALL NATIVEWIND: Run `yarn add nativewind tailwindcss react-native-css-interop`.
2. SETUP TAILWIND: Create `tailwind.config.js` in /app/frontend/ and configure it for content: ['./app/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}', './components/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}'].
3. ICONS: Use `@expo/vector-icons` (Ionicons or MaterialCommunityIcons) which is already installed.
4. NAVIGATION: Use `expo-router` (already installed). Create tabs in `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`.
5. SHADCN-LIKE: Build 'primitive' components (Card, Button, Input) in `components/ui/` using nativewind classes. Do not try to install web-only shadcn/ui.
6. API: Backend is FastAPI. Ensure proper `fetch` calls to `http://10.0.2.2:8000` (Android Emulator) or localhost.
7. UI RULES: Keep text large (min 16px). Use Icons heavily. No gradients on text.
universal_guidelines: Target audience: Low digital literacy. Make buttons BIG.
Language support: Prepare UI for dynamic string lengths (Hindi/Tamil strings can be long).
